meta.title: Making Finance Invisible: Building the Killer App for the Tokenized Economy

transcript:
Jacquelyn Melinek: Hi everyone, I am Jacquelyn Melinek, as they've mentioned. I am the Founder and CEO of Token Relations, and I am joined today with Mark Greenberg, the Global Head of Consumer at Kraken. Mark, how are you?

Mark Greenberg: I am awesome. This is such a fun event here in Abu Dhabi, and looking forward to this chat.

Jacquelyn Melinek: 100%. So the title of this panel is extremely timely because we've seen so many breakout apps going mainstream, I would say this year mostly, by making the tech invisible. What do you think is kind of the secret sauce there? Because I know you probably thought of a few apps that have really hit that mainstream adoption, so what do you think is making that so successful?

Mark Greenberg: Yeah, it's a great question. I would say you actually said some of it: making the tech invisible and behind the scenes is a big part of that. It's weird, I've spent my whole career in banking trying to make banks suck less, and a big part of why banks suck is everything is hard and you have to really understand the specifics of it. And when you think about something like equities, it's even worse. You have so many steps and processes and things you have to do. And that is what I think all of the apps that we've been seeing today and yesterday, and all of the things we're building with X-Stocks behind the scenes, really allow. They allow us to just make trading of equities, make trading of everything, make buying of everything simple, so that banking doesn't have to suck anymore.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Yeah, I mean, you already beat me to my next question, but how do you kind of think about this from the tokenization front? Because like you said, it's very different when financial apps or trading is involved, because you can't really shake away the tech side of it, but you also don't want it to be like in their face.

Mark Greenberg: Yeah, um, it's actually why I hate permissioned tokens generally and permissioned tokenization. Part of what... so when we started thinking about X-Stocks, we went and we spoke with hundreds of different organizations out there who were building things in the space. And always it was like, "Well, you can kind of put this in a wallet, but you can't actually trade it unless you get permission," or "You can only trade it to these people," or "You can only trade it on a Tuesday." You know, all these types of very specific, very complicated pieces that actually didn't make it easier. In a lot of ways, it actually put the tech in the front again and required users to go through all sorts of hoops. When we started thinking about X-Stocks, we knew we wanted it to be permissionless, 24/7, available everywhere. And that allows you to just trade it the same way you trade crypto or you trade Fartcoin or you trade anything. And so you don't have to think about it anymore. And then you can put those kinds of experiences into an app like Telegram. You can open your Telegram app and just buy a stock. You don't have to think about what time of day it is, do I have to KYC, do I have to do all this kind of work. You can just do that, and you can do it all day, all night. You can send it to your non-custodial wallet, you can do all sorts of things with it. So.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Yeah. Are you trading Fartcoin?

Mark Greenberg: I... I'm trading all sorts of meme coins. That's part of the fun.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Not a yes or a no. Very political answer.

Mark Greenberg: Yes. Of course, yes.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Okay. Just wanted to make sure I needed to carry on the conversation. So Kraken acquired Backed at the beginning of the month, bringing X-Stocks to a new market, I would argue. So how does that kind of bring this tokenized equity side into a digital economy that we haven't seen before? What's the new edge?

Mark Greenberg: So, I was on the Solana stage in New York a few months ago, and when we announced X-Stocks there, it was Backed and us on stage together. This is a product we've built fully together from day one. Something that we've been excited about, something that they've been excited about. As the product has grown—we're now at $12 billion of transactions, about 80,000 users—we just recognized that there was more we could do together if we were one organization, not two. And so this acquisition is just more of the same: more markets, more opportunities, more different companies and developers participating on this infrastructure that we're building. And more and more, we're just trying to get out of the way, build the infrastructure, and let everybody in this room and in many other rooms build on top of it in any way they want. My favorite story on that: the very first X-Stocks billboard had nothing to do with Kraken and nothing to do with Backed. A company in South Africa realized that they could use X-Stocks to sell to their client base in South Africa, and they put up an X-Stocks billboard in Johannesburg for their clients. That's the kind of thing we're trying to build with X-Stocks. The more we can be infrastructure that anyone can use in any way they want to use, the happier I am. And that means we got to be permissionless, we got to be just another SPL token that you can do anything you want with.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Do you know what the billboard said?

Mark Greenberg: Uh, "Buy stocks with X-Stocks." Very simple.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Oh, amazing. Clear. What about the creative opportunity then? Like on that note, it was like so straight to the point. But I think sometimes being creative when you're building these apps for mainstream audiences and consumers in general, you want to have that edge. And I know I already said edge, but like I think that's like a big part of this is because like there's so many things out there and you want to stand out. So how do you think about that from a creative standpoint?

Mark Greenberg: Well, for me, I recognize that we're going to, at Kraken, build one relatively straightforward experience around the way you buy and sell crypto, the way you buy and sell equities with X-Stocks. And that'll be fine for a number of users. We think it'll be an amazing experience, but it won't be for everyone. Creativity comes from decentralization and a lot of different types of folks being able to create what they want to create. And if you create this kind of infrastructure, we hope that all sorts of companies out there will do a way better job than us at selling X-Stocks and making X-Stocks part of their own applications, making their own money off of X-Stocks, and bringing their own creativity. So more and more, I hope as we get on this Solana stage more and more over the years, that everybody just by default has X-Stocks, has equities in their products, the same way that we're talking about all sorts of other tokens today.

Jacquelyn Melinek: This might be a silly question, but Kraken is a centralized exchange, then there's Ink, which is the blockchain, right? So how does that kind of play into the positioning of working with, you know, these on-chain stocks so to speak, and like what is the relationship there for centralized versus decentralized and everything?

Mark Greenberg: So, there's a place for centralized, there's a place for decentralized. Kraken plays in both. X-Stocks play in both. Um, there are ways to sell these things from fiat in Europe that are better done on a centralized exchange that has MiFID, you know, all the boring regulatory stuff. And then there's going to be lots of ways that you can buy and sell on-chain, you know, on all sorts of different apps around the world, including Telegram. Um, both will have a place to play. Kraken launched... we, Kraken and Backed, launched X-Stocks on Solana because of how amazing the Solana ecosystem is and how many different types of apps and experiences and teams and DeFi protocols there are. And, uh, we'll build some of our own on Ink, we'll build some of our own on Solana as well. And the more we can be, again, boring maybe, but infrastructure should be multichain, it should be everywhere, and it should be easy for anyone to use in any way they want, whether that's in a more centralized or decentralized way.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Yeah. On the front of Solana, what do you kind of see as like the biggest untapped potential right now for, you know, apps in this whole token economy?

Mark Greenberg: I look forward to less apps that are solely focused on a crypto audience and selling to a crypto audience, and instead are trying to make banking and investment more generally suck less. And that comes with all of us putting all the creativity that we've built in this breeding ground in crypto, bringing all of that—that is now so easy and so simple to use and there's so many amazing opportunities out there for how you buy, sell, trade crypto—how do we bring that to the rest of financial services? And what you see... I don't know, we were just watching behind stage some of the folks before us, and they're doing exactly that: bringing the best of Solana and the best of how crypto is operated to some historically very boring businesses like credit cards. So, very cool.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Yeah, so I think that's a good follow-up question is to the audience. Obviously, we have a lot of builders here. So what role do you really ask them to play in kind of expanding the influence and opportunity for, you know, capital markets infrastructure and building these killer apps?

Mark Greenberg: Yeah, it's really simple. Just think about stocks via X-Stocks the same way you think about any other token in your app. Um, we have a lot of... you know, on Kraken, we got Kraken App now, which is our neobank app experience. And you can actually use your credit card straight from Tesla stock now. So yes, you can use Bitcoin, yes you can use Solana, yes you can use any other token you want, but also you can use the S&P 500 stock or a Tesla stock. And if I think about all the builders here, I would just think about exactly the same thing. You now have 24/7 SPL-native tokens that represent stocks. Think about how you can build that into all of your experiences and apps and algorithms and DeFi protocols that you're doing. Uh, it comes mostly by default now, which is really cool. And so just think about how you build that into your experiences.

Jacquelyn Melinek: I love it. On the front of X-Stocks specifically, is there anything that is not being done right now that you would love to be, or you would love to see Backed implement?

Mark Greenberg: I'm looking forward to increasing yield opportunities on stocks. So you get the dividend by default, but how do we actually create even bigger opportunities for folks? I'm looking forward to, as we start to build revenue off of X-Stocks, giving it back to users and apps and experiences, and we're just thinking through the best way to create those kind of programs. And I just look forward to more apps that aren't financial apps by default supporting stocks. Telegram's, you know, like allows someone in Uzbekistan to buy stocks for the very first time via X-Stocks without having to jump through 10,000 individual hoops in order to get those. Maybe they only want $5, maybe they want $10, maybe they want $100. They can do that as they see fit. And I look forward to way more apps for way more markets with that in mind.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Do you find that the traditional financial market as well as traditional investors are ready to kind of adopt this type of technology?

Mark Greenberg: I think like crypto, it will take time and it will come slowly and then quickly. I'd say $12 billion of transactions so far in four or five months is definitely bigger than we were expecting, which is great news. And more and more conversations with traditional neobanks, traditional banks, you know, like some of the largest in the world, they're all trying... some of the exchanges. We just launched a partnership with Deutsche Borse in Germany. Um, all sorts of different players starting to come to the table and getting excited about X-Stocks. So yes, tentatively toe in the water for right now, but a lot more coming over time.

Jacquelyn Melinek: When will we hit a trillion?

Mark Greenberg: I... no prediction market. End of 2026, I think X-Stocks will be tens of billions of dollars of value. Actually, here's an interesting stat: we're already 0.1% of all the Tesla stock in the world is in X-Stocks. When will we be 1%? When will we be 10%? That's coming a lot sooner than folks think, and it's coming in 2026, I think, a lot of those numbers.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Okay, then 100% one day.

Mark Greenberg: 100%... uh, no-brainer at some point. Like why wouldn't you use a permissionless, 24/7 version of a stock? Uh, like why would you go back to 24/5? I don't really get it.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Yeah. Yeah, who needs a life outside of trading?

Mark Greenberg: It's not a life outside of trading. It's actually just as simple as like, why do I have to... you know, like actually this happens... this used to happen to me all the time. I work during the day, Eastern hours. Get home at whatever, 9, 10 p.m. You look at your portfolio, and then you can't do anything with it.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Can't do anything, yeah.

Mark Greenberg: How silly... or like on the weekend when I actually have some time to like do some research and understand things. Oh, by the way, I can't actually do anything with it. It's not that I plan to trade 24/7 personally, but the fact that you can just opens up a lot more opportunities. Also, if you live in a market like this one and you want to trade US stocks, the liquid markets on the traditional market are only during the day, which is like basically overnight here. So makes it really really hard to trade unless you're... uh, if you can't go 24/7. And there's no reason in this world you can't.

Jacquelyn Melinek: 100%. Okay, in the near term and long term, Mark, how do you kind of think people will fundamentally change the way they interact with money, markets, payments? What does that fusion and evolution look like to you?

Mark Greenberg: I look forward to a world where you don't need your local fiat currency anymore. Everything can be money. As long as it's liquid, as long as it's available 24/7, as long as you can do it in any fraction you want, everything can be money. So why as a Canadian must I predominantly hold Canadian dollars? Why can't I hold my value in US dollars or US stablecoins or the S&P 500? And I look forward to a world where all of those things are seen as money. And the new Kraken cards that we just launched are our first vision towards that, where you can basically use more than 500 different things when you pay. That gets me really excited. I want to get that to a thousand and ten thousand and a hundred thousand things, so that I don't... and actually personally, I don't hold much Canadian dollar anymore, which is a fantastic, uh, uh, spot to be in. Why wouldn't I be able to just hold it in the S&P 500, which is now what I do.

Jacquelyn Melinek: You don't own a lot of Canadian dollars? What do you own, stablecoins?

Mark Greenberg: Stablecoins, but actually more... uh, some ETFs actually. So you can put it in an X-Stock ETF, and then I can spend directly from it. So I don't have to hold just dollars anymore.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Amazing. Alright, last thing, Mark. If you could give us the perfect future of what global market access looks like in five years, what would that look like to you?

Mark Greenberg: It's really simple. Everyone has access in the same way to the same things across all asset classes, and everybody can access them 24/7 permissionless. I think that's the dream of a lot of people in the crypto world, and we have to bring that to more than just crypto assets, to everything that we... to stocks and then to a lot more asset classes over time.

Jacquelyn Melinek: Amazing. Mark, thank you so much, and thank you to everyone for listening in.

December 2, 2025|[News](https://blog.kraken.com/category/news)
# Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities
The Backed acquisition strengthens our tokenized asset leadership and marks a key milestone toward making global capital markets open, accessible and borderless.
### Share this:
* [Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=twitter)
* [Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=facebook)
* ![](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/xStocks-Backed-Annoucement-Blog.png?fit=3070%2C1400&ssl=1)
We are excited to announce we have agreed to acquire[Backed Finance AG](https://backed.fi/), the company driving the issuance of[xStocks](https://xstocks.com/), the leading industry standard for tokenized equities. The acquisition will accelerate the global adoption of xStocks; unifying issuance, trading and settlement to power open capital markets infrastructure for everyone.
xStocks has seen exceptional adoption since its debut earlier in 2025, surpassing $10 billion in combined exchange and onchain trading volume within six months. This acquisition gives Backed access to the capital and resources needed to scale faster and capitalize on surging adoption and growing institutional demand for tokenized equities.
Bringing Backed fully into our ecosystem also allows greater alignment on strategy and investment priorities, unlocking the full economic potential of xStocks.
Together, Kraken and Backed will accelerate xStocks’ expansion, bringing tokenized equities to new markets and extending their reach into everyday financial experiences. The acquisition lays the groundwork for future innovation in tokenized assets beyond equities, advancing our leadership in building open, interoperable financial infrastructure.
xStocks currently offers exposure to more than 60 tokenized equities and ETFs backed 1:1 by the underlying asset. The tokens are live on Solana and Ethereum, with integrations on TON, Tron, Mantle and BNB Chain coming soon.
Investors can trade 24 hours a day, five days a week on Kraken, or directly onchain 24/7, creating a seamless, continuous bridge between traditional and crypto markets. xStocks can be self-custodied and were designed for multi-chain composability, keeping the infrastructure open and usable across the broader ecosystem.
Through the xStocks Alliance, Kraken and Backed have built a thriving ecosystem of partners spanning blockchain foundations, trading venues, and consumer applications. With this acquisition, we plan to further expand this ecosystem, enhancing interoperability and liquidity for tokenized equities across the industry.
**Arjun Sethi, Kraken Co-CEO**: “Integrating Backed into Kraken strengthens the core architecture required for open and programmable capital markets. Unifying issuance, trading and settlement under one framework ensures the infrastructure for tokenized assets remains transparent, reliable and globally accessible. This is foundational work for the next era of market structure. This is bigger than giving people exposure to U.S. equities, it’s about redefining what it means to own assets in the digital era.”
Backed’s team and infrastructure will become part of Kraken, strengthening issuance and compliance capabilities while expanding the reach and utility of tokenized assets beyond the crypto ecosystem

November 12, 2025|[xstocks](https://blog.kraken.com/category/product/xstocks)
# xStocks surpasses $10 billion in total transaction volume, with nearly $2 billion onchain
The gold standard for tokenized equities crosses a major milestone less than six months after launch, underscoring explosive global demand for open, permissionless and interoperable tokenized assets.
### Share this:
* [Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X](https://blog.kraken.com/product/xstocks/10-billion-in-total-transaction?share=twitter)
* [Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook](https://blog.kraken.com/product/xstocks/10-billion-in-total-transaction?share=facebook)
* ![](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/xStocksxBacked_Blog_Hero_1535x700%402x.jpg?fit=3070%2C1400&ssl=1)
## **A milestone that signals growing global adoption**
[xStocks](https://www.kraken.com/xstocks), the industry’s gold standard for tokenized U.S. equities developed by Kraken and[Backed](https://backed.fi/), has surpassed $10 billion in combined centralized and decentralized exchange transaction volume, including nearly $2 billion in onchain activity.
Achieved in just 135 days since public launch, the milestone reflects surging global appetite for open, permissionless, interoperable financial assets that connect traditional capital markets to the open internet economy.
## **Expanding access to tokenized equities**
With xStocks, people in eligible regions can gain exposure to[U.S. stocks and ETFs](https://www.kraken.com/stocks), hold assets on platforms like Kraken or self-custodial wallets, and unlock a wide range of DeFi opportunities using their holdings as collateral.
Initially launched on[Solana](https://www.kraken.com/learn/what-is-solana-sol)and made available to Kraken clients in more than 160 countries and Bybit users, xStocks have since expanded to[Ethereum](https://www.kraken.com/learn/what-is-ethereum-eth), with upcoming integrations extending access and liquidity across blockchains and platforms.
## **Building a global network of partners**
Beyond Kraken, xStocks are powering a rapidly growing ecosystem of global partners —including Alchemy Pay, Bybit, Gate.io, Phantom Wallet,[Trust Wallet](https://blog.kraken.com/product/xstocks/trust-wallet),[Wallet in Telegram](https://blog.kraken.com/product/xstocks/wallet-in-telegram)and others.
Together, these integrations form a thriving multi-platform alliance that connects traditional equities to the open internet economy, allowing users to buy, trade and transfer tokenized stocks seamlessly across networks, wallets and exchanges.
## **Bridging traditional markets and onchain innovation**
**Mark Greenberg, Kraken Global Head of Consumer**, said “xStocks have bridged the worlds of crypto and traditional investing, turning the promise of tokenized equities into a global reality. Crossing this milestone so quickly proves that people around the world want financial assets that live natively on the internet –permissionless, open and accessible to anyone. xStocks are redefining how real-world assets can move, trade and thrive onchain.”
## **Fully backed and transparently structured**
Each xStock is fully backed 1:1 by its underlying equity or ETF, held by a licensed custodian in a bankruptcy-remote structure. Tokens are issued by Backed, in partnership with Kraken, which provides distribution, capital and liquidity –combining regulatory rigor with crypto-native interoperability.
## **Growing participation and real-world asset momen

tags.impact_reason: Fireside chat discussing X-Stocks and tokenization of equities, highlighting Kraken's involvement, represents a solid update within the RWA space.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a vintage stock certificate as the semantic anchor because it's the most direct physical representation of the 'equity' or 'Real World Asset' at the heart of the story. Showing it deconstruct into crystalline blocks visually explains the process of 'tokenization'. I included the text '0.1%' because it's a hard data point from the article that grounds this futuristic concept in reality, highlighting that this transformation is already happening with major companies like Tesla. Integrating it as a holographic etching makes it part of the high-tech world.
visual_idea.description: In a dark, cavernous data-vault, a single vintage Tesla stock certificate floats, dramatically lit from above. The bottom half of the paper certificate is disintegrating, transforming into a cascade of sharp, glowing cyan crystalline blocks that represent the blockchain. These digital blocks flow downwards like liquid currency. Holographically etched onto the facet of a larger, semi-translucent crystal is the high-impact text '0.1%'. The background is deep and atmospheric, with racks of servers and glowing fiber-optic cables receding into the volumetric haze, suggesting massive global infrastructure.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, high-angle composition in a dark, cavernous data-vault, emphasizing verticality. A single vintage Tesla stock certificate floats majestically, dramatically illuminated from above by a powerful, cinematic volumetric light beam. The bottom half of the paper certificate is undergoing a stunning disintegration, seamlessly transforming into a dynamic cascade of sharp, glowing cyan crystalline blocks. These digital blocks, representing the blockchain, flow downwards with a liquid-like currency effect. Adjacent, a larger, semi-translucent crystal features the high-impact text '0.1%' meticulously holographic-etched onto its facet, casting subtle glow. The background recedes into a deep, atmospheric volumetric haze, revealing vast racks of servers and intricate glowing fiber-optic cables, suggesting a massive global infrastructure. The overall scene is ultra-photorealistic with hyper-detailed textures and realistic materials, enhanced by cinematic contrast and natural color grading. Emphasize atmospheric lighting with volumetric fog and chiaroscuro. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, incredibly intricate, sharp focus, monumental scale, epic, vertical composition 3:4.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a vintage stock certificate as the semantic anchor because it's the most direct physical representation of the 'equity' or 'Real World Asset' at the heart of the story. Showing it deconstruct into crystalline blocks visually explains the process of 'tokenization'. I included the text '0.1%' because it's a hard data point from the article that grounds this futuristic concept in reality, highlighting that this transformation is already happening with major companies like Tesla. Integrating it as a holographic etching makes it part of the high-tech world.
visual_idea.description: A tight, macro shot focusing on the point of transformation. We see the textured fibers and engraved typography of the vintage stock certificate on the left, seamlessly fracturing and transitioning into the sharp, geometric, and internally luminous crystalline blocks on the right. The lighting creates a stark, beautiful contrast between the analog warmth of the paper and the cool, digital precision of the tokenized asset. No text.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A captivating, tight, macro shot, perfectly centered and symmetrical, filling the entire 1:1 square frame with intricate texture and detail, focusing intently on a pivotal point of transformation. On the left, a vintage stock certificate with visible textured fibers and deeply engraved typography, bathed in a warm, soft analog light, begins to seamlessly fracture. This organic paper texture dramatically transitions and fragments into sharp, precise, geometric, and internally luminous crystalline blocks on the right. These crystalline structures, reminiscent of holographic data, glow with a cool, digital luminescence, reflecting light with architectural precision. Cinematic volumetric lighting creates a stark and beautiful contrast: warm, golden light illuminates the aged paper, while cool, blue-white light emanates from and reflects off the tokenized crystalline asset, casting dramatic shadows and highlighting every hyper-detailed surface. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic volumetric lighting, depth of field.

editorial.headline: Kraken Acquires Backed to Scale $12 Billion Tokenized Stock Market
editorial.standfirst: The vertical integration of the Swiss issuer signals a pivot from experimental RWAs to permissionless global infrastructure. With 0.1% of Tesla’s supply already onchain, Kraken is betting that equities will soon function as liquid currency.
key_takeaways: Kraken has officially acquired **Backed**, vertically integrating the issuance of X-Stocks to accelerate expansion into new markets.
**X-Stocks** transaction volume surged to $12 billion with 80,000 users in five months, now holding 0.1% of all **Tesla** shares in circulation.
New integrations allow permissionless stock trading via **Telegram** and direct spending of **S&P 500** assets via Kraken cards, bypassing fiat entirely.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative around Real World Assets (RWAs) has abruptly shifted from theoretical pilots to aggressive vertical integration. **Kraken’s** acquisition of **Backed**, the Swiss-based issuer behind **X-Stocks**, represents a calculated move to own the full stack of tokenized equity infrastructure. This is no longer about offering a niche crypto product; it is about replacing the friction-heavy legacy banking rails with invisible, permissionless technology. With transaction volumes hitting $12 billion in under six months, the market has validated the demand for 24/7 equity trading that operates outside the constraints of traditional market hours.

Mark Greenberg, Global Head of Consumer at **Kraken**, argues that the "killer app" for tokenization is invisibility. By embedding stock trading into everyday interfaces like **Telegram** and enabling direct spending via debit cards, the friction of traditional brokerage accounts evaporates. The alpha here is not just in trading fees, but in the utility of the asset itself. When a user in Uzbekistan can buy $5 of **Tesla** without KYC hoops, or a Canadian can spend fractionalized **S&P 500** shares for coffee, the distinction between "investment asset" and "currency" begins to collapse.

The implications for **Solana** are significant. **Kraken** chose the network for its ability to handle high-frequency, low-latency transactions essential for this "everything is money" thesis. As Greenberg noted, **X-Stocks** already commands 0.1% of the total global supply of **Tesla** shares—a figure he expects to scale rapidly toward 1% and beyond by 2026. This suggests a future where liquidity fragmentation between traditional finance and DeFi is solved not by bridges, but by absorbing traditional assets directly onto the blockchain as SPL tokens.
